competition.narrative: No active vendor-vs-vendor evaluation is underway; the primary competitor is 'Do Nothing / Status Quo.' Robinsons Land recently transitioned to SAP S/4HANA and currently has no procurement platform—sourcing (RFI/RFX/RFP) is manual, and contract/supplier/spend management are not systematized beyond SAP PO-level visibility. The 19 May 2026 demo (~6 weeks ago) confirmed the buyer's current state ('RFI, RFX, r it's not in the system yet… it's the manual thing'), and no competitor was mentioned in any call, Next_Step, Task, or Salesforce field. The risk is inertia: the buyer may defer procurement-platform investment until the SAP S/4HANA transition stabilizes, IT/procurement bandwidth is freed, and a compelling event (cost overrun, compliance failure, or executive mandate) creates urgency. The SAP transition itself may be consuming resources and delaying modernization decisions. Our wedge is to position Zycus as the natural next step post-SAP-stabilization—native SAP S/4HANA integration, multi-entity support, and agentic AI that drives immediate tail-spend savings and adoption without requiring heavy IT lift. The activation is to co-develop a phased roadmap with Jennifer: pilot Merlin Intake and tail-spend automation in Q4 2026 (low-risk, high-visibility quick win), then scale to full Source-to-Pay in 2027 once SAP is stable.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is implied but not explicitly quantified or owned. The 19 May 2026 demo (~6 weeks ago) framed several challenges Zycus addresses: (1) 'multiple workforce generations require an easier, more guided user interface to drive adoption'—suggesting Robinsons Land struggles with procurement-system complexity and user resistance; (2) tail-spend inefficiency and lack of audit trails; (3) manual RFI/RFX/RFP processes not yet in a system (buyer confirmed 'it's the manual thing… RFI, RFX, r it's not in the system yet' during the demo); (4) decentralized procurement across business units (hotels/resorts, construction, operational procurement) with no standardized platform, though SAP provides PO-level visibility. The pain owner is Jennifer Ursolino (Director - Cost Control Procurement), but she has not articulated a compelling event, cost-of-inaction, or ROI target that would create urgency. The recent SAP S/4HANA transition may be consuming IT/procurement bandwidth, deferring procurement-platform modernization. The next move is to co-develop a quantified pain narrative with Jennifer: what does the manual sourcing/contract/tail-spend process cost today (time, FTE burden, missed savings, compliance risk), and what outcome would justify the investment?

deltas.to: Map and engage the Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Finance) immediately. Jennifer (Director procurement) led the 19 May demo and is the Decision Maker on record, but
deltas.subject: Map and engage the Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Finance) immediately. Jennifer (Director procurement) led the 19 May demo and is the Decision Maker on record, but she almost certainly lacks final $200k commercial sign-off authority for a multi-module platform. No CFO, VP Finance, or C-level sponsor has been identified or engaged. This is the single highest-probability blocker: without an Economic Buyer mapped and bought-in, the deal will stall at proposal or be deprioritized when Jennifer escalates internally. The play: ask Jennifer directly on the next call 'Who will approve the budget and sign the contract - is that you, or does this go to your CFO or a VP Finance?' Secure an introduction, then run an executive value conversation anchored to a co-built ROI model (once created) and a peer reference (SM or BDO). Make this the rank-1 priority before any other sales motion.

deltas.to: Build a quantified ROI case and a compelling event with Jennifer to create urgency. The 19 May demo showed strong product fit (SAP integration, Merlin Intake, A
deltas.subject: Build a quantified ROI case and a compelling event with Jennifer to create urgency. The 19 May demo showed strong product fit (SAP integration, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, unified S2P), but the buyer has not translated that interest into a quantified business case, a cost-of-inaction, or a forcing function. Without a documented savings target, cycle-time improvement, or compliance-risk reduction tied to a CFO mandate or audit finding, the buyer may defer the purchase or allocate budget elsewhere. The play: co-author a savings model with Jennifer anchored to their decentralized manual procurement pain (hours saved, tail-spend savings, compliance risk mitigated), tie it to a compelling event (e.g., a planned centralization initiative, a CFO cost-reduction mandate, or an upcoming budget cycle), and present it to the Economic Buyer for sign-off. This moves the deal from 'interesting demo' to 'board-level priority'.

deltas.to: Multi-thread beyond Jennifer to IT leadership and Finance to derisk single-point-of-failure and accelerate buyer alignment. Jennifer is the sole engaged Decisio
deltas.subject: Multi-thread beyond Jennifer to IT leadership and Finance to derisk single-point-of-failure and accelerate buyer alignment. Jennifer is the sole engaged Decision Maker; all other contacts (Charlotte, Jomar, Melvin, Malou) are Influencers who attended the 19 May demo but have not been engaged individually since. SAP S4HANA integration is a confirmed buyer priority, yet the IT leaders (Jomar Geronimo Group Manager IT, Melvin Dela Cruz IT Manager Operations) who own the SAP environment and integration approvals have not been threaded separately. Rep follow-up 6 Jun to Jennifer received no reply in 25 days, signaling her engagement is thinning or she is deprioritizing. The play: (1) reach out directly to Jomar and Melvin with a targeted offer: 'Would a 30-min technical deep-dive on our SAP S4HANA connectors and integration architecture be useful for your team?' (2) Ask Jennifer to introduce the Rep to Finance (VP Finance or CFO) to co-present the ROI case once built. Multi-threading creates redundancy if Jennifer cools, accelerates internal alignment, and surfaces blockers (IT concerns, Finance budget constraints) early.

Without a documented savings target, cycle-time improvement, or compliance-risk reduction tied to a CFO mandate or audit finding, the buyer may defer the purchase or allocate budget elsewhere. This moves the deal from 'interesting demo' to 'board-level priority'.
